description: 'napa' covers food made by combining ground grain or another meal with liquid, shaping the mixture, and cooking it. The word includes leavened and unleavened breads, flatbreads, and loaves. 'napuro' (grain) is the seed or harvested material before that work.
articulatory_notes: Touch the tongue tip to the upper teeth for voiced dental nasal 'n' and open into stressed 'a'. Close both lips for unvoiced 'p', release them cleanly, and finish on a second open 'a'.
sound_symbolism: The shared opening of 'napa' and 'napuro' (grain) makes their relation easy to hear. The shorter contour can recall grain gathered into finished bread.
usage_notes: Bread as a substance needs no classifier. A counted loaf may insert optional 'themo': 'ta themo napa' is one loaf, while 'ta napa' remains grammatical when context already supplies the unit.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Bread joins grain, water, heat, time, and practised judgement. A recipe can guide the hands, but the dough still answers to weather, flour, and fermentation on that day.

semantic_domains.creation: a staple food made by mixing, shaping, and cooking meal or flour
semantic_domains.physical: cooked grain food handled as a loaf, piece, or substance
